statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, find and return the duplicate element present in the array. If no duplicate is found, it should return -1.
statement.outputFormat: Duplicate element or -1 if no duplicate is found
statement.notes: The array can have duplicates. The length of the array is guaranteed to be between 1 and 10,000.

examples.explanation: There is a duplicate at index 3
There is a duplicate at index 3

hints.text: We can solve this problem by using the two-pointer technique.

editorial.title: Solution Overview
editorial.intuition: Use two pointers, one at the beginning and one at the end of the array. Compare the elements pointed by the two pointers and increment them accordingly.
editorial.bruteForce: This problem can be solved by iterating through the array using a single pointer and checking for duplicates in each iteration.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the two-pointer technique as described in the intuition.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the two-pointer technique and increment the pointers based on the comparison of the elements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the fact that the two-pointer technique correctly identifies the duplicate element.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: 1) Initialize two pointers, one at the beginning and one at the end of the array. 2) Compare the elements pointed by the two pointers. 3) If they match, increment the pointer that was pointing to the smaller element. 4) If they don't match, increment the pointer that was pointing to the larger element.
